question_text: Which of the following is an example of gravitational potential energy?

options.label: A
options.text: A ball rolling along the ground

options.label: B
options.text: A book sitting on a shelf above the ground

options.label: D
options.text: A light bulb that is switched on

correct_answer: A book sitting on a shelf above the ground
explanation: Gravitational potential energy is the energy stored in an object because of its height above the ground. The higher an object is positioned, the more gravitational potential energy it has. A book on a shelf has gravitational potential energy because it is above ground level and could fall, releasing that energy.
